programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In March 2015, football fan Simon Dobbin was at an away game when he was set upon by a gang of men in an unprovoked attack that left him brain damaged, paralysed and unable to walk and talk. After many months in hospital he is finally back at home in Mildenhall Suffolk, where he receives round-the-clock care from wife Nicole. But the family home is unfit for Simon's care and rehabilitation - so that's where Nick Knowles, designer Laurence Llewelyn- Bowen and the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Prime Minister Folami declares a public holiday for the annual midsummer festival, to promote integration and quell the unrest - so Kamal plots to cause maximum chaos by ordering the Mercy Point cadets to stand patrol during the celebrations, hoping their presence will anger the festival-goers. Lekan makes it very clear to Callum that he wants him out - he is just waiting for the right moment - while the Hadleys cannot understand why Sephy is acting so differently, only for things to take a nasty turn when Meggie interferes in her friends' personal business(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Weston-super-Mare, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley goes out and about the south-east of England monitoring how police officers are taking on the crooks. She talks to fire officers who are appealing to the public to 'phone rather than film' when witnessing a fire. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A poorly planned attempt at a frozen fraud melts away when investigators turn up the heat, while a prisoner claiming for injury behind bars forgets that the camera never lies. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool's St Georges Hall. Host Eric Knowles crosses the Mersey to find out about the world's finest collection of Della Robbia pottery(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Valerie go to great lengths to exonerate Jimmi, only for him to be betrayed by a friend on the inside(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in south-west England to help a creative couple with a £805,000 budget escape their high-pressure jobs and buy a house on the Jurassic Coast from where they can run a luxury camping business. While in the area, Jonnie visits Britain's only thatched brewery which has been making ales for over 200 years, and learns from a family descendent how traditional brewing methods are still very much in use today(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are called in by a young family in Fishponds, Bristol, to create a garden to unwind in after work. Zoe and Jonny are dedicated charity workers who want and need a sanctuary spot to relax away from their emotionally challenging work. They also want the designers to find a way of including a place for Zoe to grow her wedding bouquet for their upcoming marriage, somewhere for new baby, Ottilie, to play and even an outdoor spot for Zoe to practise alfresco yoga(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Colleagues Dean and Julian are paired with chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie, with fish likely to be on the menu as both work for a charity supporting fishing communities. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Kym Marsh round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste examine criminals extorting money from unsuspecting victims from online dating scams, beginning with a man who tricked a businesswoman out of a total of almost £60,000 over the course of a few months. As they unravel the evidence that allowed the fraudster to appear so plausible, a dramatic showdown with the conman awaits. Plus, a man reveals what it's like to have personal photos stolen and used as part of an online 'catfishing' campaign, and an extraordinary international con is exposed(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson, former Premier League footballer Matt Holland, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black and ex-England cricketer Isa Guha join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After learning of Ben's arrest, Callum is distraught when Kathy reveals Ben was leaving the Prince Albert with another man and makes a difficult decision. Linda is unnerved when Shelley arrives at the Vic and tells Isaac about the landlady's drinking. Tiffany seeks advice from Callum over her problems with Keegan(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic Tom Parker Bowles sets the brief for the penultimate quarter-final, challenging the hopefuls to serve up a dish featuring chilli. John Torode and Gregg Wallace then decide who will be progressing further in the competition and who will be hanging up their aprons(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress and writer Daisy May Cooper, quiz guru Richard Osman, actress, comedian and writer Diane Morgan, and journalist and broadcaster Rory Reid join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: In Witley, Surrey, Sarah Moore calls on blacksmith Kev Paxton to revamp a pair of metal bed ends, while upholsterer Ray Clarke gives an old armchair a complete redesign(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Akis Petretzikis and Romy Gill cook meals in minutes with two competitive siblings from Liverpool. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the east of England en route to Lincolnshire, discovering how the Crossrail project is helping to conserve birds and taking a gelato cart to Southend(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Clovelly in Devon, looking at its transformation from fishing harbour to romantic Victorian seaside resort. Thanks to a powerful and pioneering woman, Clovelly found itself at the forefront of the holiday revolution, and even today cars are banned and the village is privately owned, helping to preserve it as a reminder of a bygone age(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Includes live coverage of Prime Minister's Questions from midday(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Competing in Cardiff for a place in the final are a renovated, three-storey semi-detached, a period semi with a modern twist and a rustic, shabby-chic home with a love of upcycling(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Mason McQueen's black cab takes Terry Wogan to Conwy, on the north coast of Wales, a small medieval town with a large castle. The locals show off their cuisine, and after getting a barbecue going, Terry tempts motorists to sample his home made burgers. The taxi also heads over the Menai Straits to Anglesey, and the duo learn how to produce salt, the ancient skill of oyster farming and the importance of conservation(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Rescuing Irena has mixed results for Sean, the Kielys are split over Keith taking a trip, and a witness does a runner - or tries to(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: The two remaining designers compete for the last place in the quarter final, this time taking on the task of redesigning an iconic farmhouse kitchen. The pair have been given very different briefs, with one designer facing the challenge of painting over the dated wooden units. Although the decorative elements are well-received, the judges have concerns over the practical aspects of the design. The other contestant showcases their ingenuity with kitchen utensil light fittings, bespoke hooks and quirky accessories, but in doing so may have neglected the overall look of the French farm house brief. Presented by Tom Dyckhoff(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: EastEnders actress Natalie Cassidy talks to Brian Conley about her favourite TV shows from her childhood, including Only Fools and Horses, and University Challenge(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt are in Glemham Hall in Suffolk, where they uncover a 17th-century apothecary jar, while Paul Martin learns about a medieval port lost to the sea(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Samantha Womack, Top Gear's Rory Reid, broadcaster Anne Diamond and comedian James Acaster take part in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo arrives in Croydon, where he hears the story of a mixed-race composer who was once celebrated but is now forgotten. At the Three Bridges station, he is delighted to find a recently built depot and its fleet of new Class 700 trains - and is given the great honour of washing one down. In Lewes, he makes a beeline for Charleston, the home of artists Vanessa Bell and Duncan Grant, and in Shoreham-by-Sea, Michael discovers how magnificent Edwardians in flying machines took off from the oldest licensed airport in the country(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Lochs
programme.desc: Landing at Glenuig, Paul Murton makes his way inland to Loch Moidart and MacDonald country, walking the old coffin road, which was used for centuries by funeral parties, taking their dead to a sacred isle. On the track Paul meets a trials bike enthusiast and learns the secrets of a 'dabbing coat' before landing off the Green Isle, where he rings a 1,100-year-old bell for the dead. Taking a boat north, Paul sails up Loch Shiel to Glenfinnan where Bonnie Prince Charlie officially began his rising to reclaim the throne for the Stuart Dynasty. From a forgotten monument that commemorates this event, Paul heads back to the coast and Loch Ailort, where he learns how to knife fight before taking a trip across the loch to a deserted community on the roadless Ardnish peninsula(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The nation's top chefs compete for the chance to cook at an incredible banquet, creating dishes inspired by iconic food from children's literature, from Willy Wonka to Alice in Wonderland. This year, the contestants will prepare dishes hoping to impress not only the judges, but a series of guest judges from the world of children's literature, including David Baddiel and Jacqueline Wilson. Tonight, chefs from the Central region take part in the first of the weekly heats. Presented by Susan Calman(n)

programme.title: Taking Control: The Dominic Cummings Story
programme.desc: Emily Maitlis examines Boris Johnson's chief adviser Dominic Cummings' place in the UK's changing political landscape, stretching back over two decades. The film charts his arrival in Downing Street as a senior adviser with significant and perhaps unprecedented power. Now, at the apex of the largest Conservative majority since 1987, Cummings aims to play a key role in reshaping the nation, economy and government. Featuring testimonies from some of his fiercest critics and closest political friends, the documentary sheds light on a man whose ambitions may now direct Britain's journey for years to come(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Award-winning funnywoman Gina Yashere introduces surreal Australian comedian Sam Simmons and rising comedy star Ellie Taylor to the stage in a gig recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: A colourful hair entrepreneur offers one Dragon a new look - and all of them a new investment opportunity. A cheesecake-maker has sweet dreams of a cash boost for his franchise model, but will his pitch be the stuff of nightmares? A husband-and-wife team hope their electrical invention will make sparks fly in the Den, as rival Dragons Deborah Meaden and Sara Davies line up to try it out. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Aldo Kane reports on the illegal tiger trade in South East Asia, with the endangered big cats trafficked for products including wine and glue made from their bones. He works alongside wildlife crime investigators working the key trafficking routes in Malaysia, China, Thailand, Laos and Vietnam, and uncovers shocking footage of breeding facilities and animals being fattened for slaughter. With fewer than 4,000 tigers left in the wild, this profitable illegal enterprise threatens to wipe the species out altogether(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: Eighty-one-year-old Joe has been left with a hole in his heart after suffering a heart attack, and faces a complex procedure that carries a high-mortality rate. Ellie, 28, is one of the growing population of adults with congenital heart disease, who had devices fitted into their hearts as children, and need further procedures as they get older. Already cancelled twice before, she hopes that this time her operation to replace her pulmonary valve with keyhole surgery will go ahead, so she can begin to plan her wedding to Jade(n)

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Stories of bravery captured on phones, CCTV and dashcams, meeting those saved as well as the rescuers whose selfless acts can be the difference between life and death. In the first edition, CCTV captures the moment two boys are rescued from a rising tide and saved by a total stranger, while a community effort saves a seal being strangled to death by a rope around its neck.(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages three items from a recycling centre in Witley, including a dressing table and mirror, and vintage suitcase(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross challenges fairgoers to guess his mystery item(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit Battle Abbey in East Sussex, where items brought along include an incredibly rare Beatles guitar with an enviable history, which turns out to be one of the most valuable items ever seen on the show. Also featured are royal letters from Queen Victoria's children, a pawned diamond ring that saved a family from the poorhouse, a rare ninth-century gold coin and some valuable Chinese surprises.(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Hardball
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Ore Oduba in which contestants have to answer questions to get as far along a track as they can before a ball catches and passes them, ending their go(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three much-loved detached properties in Cardiff compete to be chosen for the final - a modern house with sea views, a country cottage, and a renovated farm house(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary quest, heading for Wells in Somerset, which is one of Britain's smallest cities. The pair visit the famous cathedral close as they begin their search for the region's most sought-after delicacies, and take in the medieval marketplace to sample cheddar cheese and sparkling cider. They also try Mendip wallfish cooked in fresh herbs and cider-flavoured chorizo, before rounding off their sojourn with a banquet fit for the abbot himself(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Rory's fraud case could affect Patricia and expose Tom Callaghan. The Kielys debate who should undertake the risky trip to France(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: It's the quarter final and the four reamining contenders are in the village of Beer in Devon, with each taking on the transformation of a cosy sitting-room in a row of coastal cottages. Tom Dyckhoff explores the community, once known as a haven for smugglers, and uncovers its far reaching architectural influence, before Sophie Robinson, Daniel Hopwood and guest judge Oliver Heath decide which three go into the semi-final(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Model-turned-designer Jo Wood tells Brian Conley about her favourite old shows, recalling a spy thriller with a twist, a female comic with attitude and a TV chef in a ball gown(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the historic venues from the series, as well as new valuations and unexpected auctions. Paul Martin uncovers the history of the border town Berwick-upon-Tweed(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Samantha Womack, Rory Reid, Anne Diamond and James Acaster continue the daily test of their general knowledge skills, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads to Chichester, West Sussex, where he encounters an Edwardian motoring duo patronised by King Edward VII. After a grand tour of their factory, he is treated to some rest and relaxation in one of their finest vehicles. Arriving in the resort of Southsea, Hampshire, Michael heads for the King's Theatre, where he treads the boards in a community production of Lads in the Village. Crossing the Solent, the presenter boards the Island Line to travel along the pier in a 1930s London Underground carriage, then hops on to the Isle of Wight heritage line bound for Wootton(n)

programme.title: MOTDx
programme.desc: Jermaine Jenas is joined by Craig Mitch, Chelcee Grimes and Reece Parkinson to take an offbeat look at the Premier League, also discussing skills, celebrities, fashion, music and culture(n)

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson examines the history of Robin Hood's Bay, the fishing village on the North Yorkshire coast, revealing tales of smuggling intrigue and bravery(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three remaining Central region chefs cook their mains and desserts, with dishes inspired by Fantastic Mr Fox, Charlie and the Chocolate Factory and Harry Potter(n)

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: The work of Liverpool's Walton Centre, the country's only specialist neurology hospital trust, as staff deal with patients suffering from the complex brain condition FND (Functional Neurological Disorder). Sixty-six-year-old Pauline has been left severely disabled by FND but there is nothing physically wrong with her body. David, a 38-year-old window cleaner, has been unable to walk more than a few steps a year. However, medical professionals are still learning about the condition - so the Walton Centre started an FND service in 2012(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Vic reveals his latest plastic surgery enhancement, three acts battle it out in Novelty Island, and Vaun tries to lure Vic away from Bob(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Miss World 1970: Beauty Queens and Bedlam
programme.desc: The story of the protest at the contest, when protestors dramatically disrupted the event and kickstarted a feminist revolution in front of an audience of millions. Hannah Berryman's film uses archive, animation and contributions from key players to offer an entertaining and thought-provoking celebration of a night of game-changing anarchy(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway journey through 1930s Britain from Canterbury to Skegness reaches Witham in Essex, where he visits the factory of the world's oldest supplier of metal-framed windows, which became popular in the Thirties. Crossing into Suffolk, he alights at Ipswich and discovers the story of a group of child refugees whose history is intertwined with that of Michael's family(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The next six amateurs try to prove themselves and impress judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, presenting them with a dish of their own design. John and Gregg will pick their two favourites, and those cooks earn themselves a place in the next round. The four remaining contestants are now challenged to cook off in a new test - The Three Ingredients Challenge - conjuring up a dish using squid, courgette and chilli(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The first quarter-final sees the six talented heat winners return to fight for a place in Knockout Week. Facing the Critic's Test, the week's best-performing amateurs take on an intimidating task, cooking to a brief set by top food critic Jay Rayner, who asks them to produce an exceptional dish incorporating stuffing. Hosted by John Torode and Gregg Wallace and narrated by India Fisher(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Experts transform and then sell three things about to be dumped at a tip, before giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of the items(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, while Paul Martin learns about Lord Lothian's key role in getting America involved in the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Arderns get a taste of running a store in the 1920s, 30s and 40s, considered the golden age of the corner shop due to the plentiful availability of packaged food. However, rationing quickly has a devastating impact on both the shop and the family's day-to-day lives, with carrot lollies and mutton bacon proving less than enticing meals. The Arderns learn how to make ice cream by hand and blend tea, as well as taking delivery of a vintage fruit machine. They also find themselves at the centre of a community party recreating the VE Day celebrations(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: Three wildcard properties enter the contest in Cardiff - a quirky terraced home, a modern renovated bath-house and a new build that was shortlisted for the Welsh Architecture Prize(n)

programme.title: Best House in Town
programme.desc: The judges return to Cardiff to give the winning homes from each category a second look, getting a fresh insight into each before deciding which one they like the most. Narrated by Marcus Garve(n)

programme.title: Terry and Mason's Great Food Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Terry Wogan and London cabbie Mason McQueen continue their culinary journey in Salisbury, where they taste lardy cakes in the historic market place and eat venison stew in a coaching inn with a very dubious past. They also visit the home of watercress, enjoy a curry cooked on a fire where the locals' earliest ancestors lived and meet a 12ft giant who has presided over the city since the 14th century(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: A nasty surprise is sprung in France, Johno puts unhealthy pressure on Little Barry, and wanting a fresh start Irena pursues the Pier pub job(n)

programme.title: The Great Interior Design Challenge
programme.desc: It's the semi-final, where three designers try and secure their place in the grand final. The challenge they have to conquer in order to get there is redesigning a sitting room each on a medieval terrace in the Cathedral city of Wells, in Somerset. Judging them are Sophie Robinson and Daniel Hopwood, but for this semi-final decision they have invited along another guest judge - designer and entrepreneur Orla Kiely. Tom Dyckhoff presents(n)

programme.title: The TV That Made Me
programme.desc: Linford Christie joins comedy star Brian Conley for a nostalgic look at the archive television that helped set him on the path to success as an Olympic sprinter, from the crazy kicks and amazing flicks of 1970s wrestling to the antics of Bob and Terry in The Likely Lads, via the world's first superstar footballer on and off the small screen(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Oxburgh Hall in Norfolk, David Harper is excited by a Polynesian war club, while Philip Serrell falls for a sewing machine dating back to 1875. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the history of the Bedingfeld family, whose members have called Oxburgh home for 500 years - and managed to keep their heads on their shoulders(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Actress Samantha Womack, Top Gear's Rory Reid, broadcaster Anne Diamond and comedian James Acaster test their general knowledge skills, with an overall champion being crowned for the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In Dorset, Michael Portillo visits Brownsea Island, where Robert Baden Powell began the scout movement in the early 20th century. Joining a group of present scouts, the presenter learns to build a bivouac and finds out what it is about scouting that still appeals to boys - and girls - today. In the New Forest, Michael searches out some unsung heroes of the First World War - the ponies who became war horses - and hears their story from a commoner who runs a stable and riding school. Then in Bournemouth, the one-time MP investigates a very British institution - the beach hut(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: The chef continues his 'journey without maps', leaving the main routes and tourist hotspots behind to embark on a personal road trip along picturesque and remote backroads. Here, he heads through France's eastern border regions of Alsace and Jura - famous for Comte cheese and home to his favourite French dish of choucroute garni. He then begins to head inland, visiting Burgundy for a memorable glass of Auxey-Duresses wine, and sampling oeuf en meurette with a difference. Following a memorable Sunday lunch near the French/Swiss border, he is inspired to recreate a traditional blueberry tart(n)

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Host Susan Calman invites the two highest-scoring Central chefs to go head to head and cook their six-course menus again. The duo need to impress formidable judges Matthew Fort, Oliver Peyton and Andi Oliver as well as guest judge, the children's author and comedian Charlie Higson. Charlie is renowned for writing the Young Bond Series, which has sold over a million copies in the UK and been translated into 24 different languages worldwide(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With the arrival of spring, Monty Don reflects on how Longmeadow has fared during an extraordinarily wet winter and gets going on a few essential tasks to get the garden into gear for the seasons to come. With the popularity of houseplants gaining huge momentum, Frances Tophill visits RHS Wisley to find out how to care for the myriad of different types of indoor plants now available to gardeners. Plus, a visit to the enthusiast who has a passion for plants in pots and curates a display of over 1,000 in her Somerset garden(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Eagles are the most powerful birds in the sky, capable of tackling enormous prey, spotting food at vast distances and soaring for miles on the wing. Armed with cutting-edge filming technology, this documentary examines the science behind such extraordinary abilities. Featuring dramatic stories and some remarkable experiments, the film follows the story of a family of bald eagles in Iowa as two chicks face the challenges of life in the wild. Plus, bird specialist Lloyd Buck puts his trained golden eagle Tilly's skills to the test(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Bill Bailey, Jan Ravens and Grayson Perry join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an unconventional look at the theme 'Over and Ova'. During the course of the show, the panellists learn some tricks with eggs, meet a flying pig and join in as the studio audience sings along with Western film themes. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: The Man with the Iron Heart (2017)
programme.desc: In 1942, two Czech resistance members prepare for a suicide mission against the Third Reich as they plan to assassinate Nazi leader Reinhard Heydrich, the mastermind of the Final Solution. Thriller, starring Jason Clarke, Rosamund Pike, Jack O'Connell and Jack Reynor(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Handmade on the Silk Road
programme.desc: An insight into the life of Shavkat Jumanijozov, who has been working with wood in Khiva, Uzbekistan, for more than 30 years, producing carved doors, chests and wooden pillars that are displayed and sold across the world. The programme captures the creation of a two metre wooden column, from the first cuts into the wood, to its carving, sanding, shaping and varnishing, and Shavkat explains how he hand built his own hotel and trained over 30 local apprentices in the art of woodcarving(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: The crafters tackle the massive dining room in their creative communal home. Leaving behind their 21st century tools and techniques they are set to craft from scratch a set of decorative Tondino plates with a particularly tricky glaze, a complete set of curtains and a pair of ornamental fire dogs to sit in the hearth. It isn't all work, however, as the crafters also find time to stage their own unique performance of a much-loved nursery rhyme and take part in some very Victorian exercise. Presented by Anita Rani and Keith Brymer Jones(n)

programme.title: Steve McQueen: Le Mans and The Man (2015)
programme.desc: Feature-length documentary following Steve McQueen's efforts to make his passion project, the 1971 film Le Mans, revealing the true extent of the troubled production process. Throughout filming, Steve would lose his marriage, close friendships, control of his film and endangered lives, as he risked everything in pursuit of his dream(n)

programme.title: Hunt v Lauda: F1's Greatest Racing Rivals
programme.desc: Documentary examining the battle between Britain's James Hunt and Austria's Niki Lauda for the 1976 Formula 1 world drivers' championship, which was decided by the last grand prix of the season. Featuring rare archive footage and interviews with team managers, families, journalists and friends(n)

programme.title: Sir Chris Hoy: 200mph at Le Mans
programme.desc: Documentary following the Olympic cyclist as he retrains as a racing driver to pursue a lifelong dream of competing in the world's toughest endurance race, the Le Mans 24 Hours. His new sporting career is nearly brought to an abrupt end when the team he has joined is forced to withdraw from the competition. He is able to rejoin the event with a new team, that has never entered Le Mans before, but torrential rain and technical troubles bring home the danger of endurance racing(n)

programme.title: Sword, Musket & Machine Gun: Britain's Armed History
programme.desc: Sam Willis traces the evolution of weaponry in Britain, beginning by getting to grips with the swords, lances and shields used on the battlefield 1,000 years ago(n)

programme.title: Virago: Changing the World One Page at a Time
programme.desc: Documentary tracing the origins and development of the publishing house founded in 1973 by Carmen Callil to promote books by female writers. Featuring contributions from Virago authors including Margaret Atwood, Sarah Waters, Sarah Dunant, Maya Angelou and Naomi Wolf as well as founding team members Carmen Callil, Ursula Owen, Harriet Spicer, Lennie Goodings and Alexandra Pringle(n)

programme.title: The Victorian House of Arts and Crafts
programme.desc: The crafters tackle the massive dining room in their creative communal home. Leaving behind their 21st century tools and techniques they are set to craft from scratch a set of decorative Tondino plates with a particularly tricky glaze, a complete set of curtains and a pair of ornamental fire dogs to sit in the hearth. It isn't all work, however, as the crafters also find time to stage their own unique performance of a much-loved nursery rhyme and take part in some very Victorian exercise. Presented by Anita Rani and Keith Brymer Jones(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Wonder of Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham explores the capabilities of bears, who can live in practically every habitat on Earth due to being able to endure extreme conditions. The naturalist examines the specialised adaptations that have enabled the mammals to thrive, including how the hollow fur of polar bears allows them to feed during gruelling winters(n)

programme.title: Shipwrecks: Britain's Sunken History
programme.desc: Maritime historian Dr Sam Willis charts the stories behind the shipwrecks of the Georgian age, when Britain's naval fleet was considered the most powerful in the world. He learns how mutinous sailors, rebellious slaves and murderous wreckers threatened to undermine the country's dominance at sea at a time when it was establishing key colonies and acquiring new territories(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The film critic uncovers the cinematic secrets of arguably the biggest genre of the moment - the superhero movie. Drawing on films stretching back more than eight decades, he reveals how these popcorn adventures tap into timeless themes and storytelling traditions, and explores the key elements of all such movies - origins, superpowers, costumes, secret identities, villains and sacrifice(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2017
programme.desc: From London's Royal Albert Hall, Katie Derham introduces a celebration of the 85th birthday of film composer John Williams. The BBC Concert Orchestra, under conductor Keith Lockhart, performs some of the best-loved music in cinema history, including movie magic from Star Wars, Harry Potter, ET and Indiana Jones as well as lesser-known gems from Williams' back catalogue(n)

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke explore the capital of Lebanon, which has rebuilt its reputation as a cosmopolitan city since the ending of the country's civil war. Their journey takes them up into the surrounding mountains by cable car to a modern Christian cathedra, while in the heart of Beirut, Janina is welcomed into an 800-year-old mosque that was once a cathedral built by Crusaders(n)

programme.title: Our Classical Century
programme.desc: The occasional series celebrating 100 years of classical music continues with its role in the nation's resistance and resilience during the Second World War. Suzy Klein and John Simpson explore rare archive footage of morale-boosting wartime National Gallery concerts by pianist Myra Hess, as well as the dramatic arrival of Shostakovich's Leningrad Symphony and its first performance at the BBC Proms. Plus, tenor Stuart Skelton singing part of Benjamin's Britten's opera Peter Grimes and the City of Birmingham Symphony Orchestra performing part of The Young Person's Guide to the Orchestra(n)

programme.title: Mark Kermode's Secrets of Cinema
programme.desc: The film critic uncovers the cinematic secrets of arguably the biggest genre of the moment - the superhero movie. Drawing on films stretching back more than eight decades, he reveals how these popcorn adventures tap into timeless themes and storytelling traditions, and explores the key elements of all such movies - origins, superpowers, costumes, secret identities, villains and sacrifice(n)

programme.title: Shipwrecks: Britain's Sunken History

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mark Goodier and Anthea Turner host the edition first shown on May 18, featuring performances by Shakin' Stevens, Deacon Blue and Neneh Cherry(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo hosts the edition first shown on May 25, featuring performances by Lynne Hamilton, Donna Summer and Edelweiss(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: Archive clips featuring solo artists, including music by performers as diverse as Sandie Shaw, Dusty Springfield, Long John Baldry, Lulu, Tom Jones, Brenton Wood, Cliff Richard, Cilla Black and Peter Sarstedt(n)

programme.title: The Story of Ready Steady Go!
programme.desc: Documentary recalling Britain's early 1960s music show Ready Steady Go!, which revolutionised television 'for the kids' and introduced emerging talent from the era. The ITV programme, synonymous with its hosts Keith Fordyce and Cathy McGowan, showcased top acts of the day including the Beatles, the Who, Sandie Shaw, Cilla Black, Otis Redding and the Rolling Stones. Contributors include original producer Vicki Wickham and pioneering director Michael Lindsay-Hogg, plus Annie Nightingale, Eric Burdon, Chris Farlowe, Mary Wilson, Martha Reeves, Paul Jones, Gerry Marsden and Jools Holland(n)

programme.title: The Best of Ready Steady Go!
programme.desc: Clips from the ITV pop show, which began in 1963 and ran until the end of 1966. The Beatles perform Twist and Shout on a moving stage, the Rolling Stones present their very own episode, while Otis Redding duets with Chris Farlowe and Eric Burdon. Other acts appearing include Cilla Black, Lulu, Martha and the Vandellas and Dusty Springfield(n)

programme.title: Roy Orbison: One of the Lonely Ones
programme.desc: Biography of the singer and musician, casting new light on the triumphs and tragedies that beset his career, featuring performances and home movies, and providing an insight into Orbison's remote Texas childhood and his battles to get his voice heard. This film reveals how his rollercoaster life was often reflected in the dark lyrics of his songs, from success to rejection, and rediscovery in the 1980s with the Travelling Wilburys supergroup. Featuring interviews with Jeff Lynne, T Bone Burnett, Bobby Goldsboro and Marianne Faithfull(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest, presented by Matt Baker and Alex Jones(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The locals anxiously await the outcome of Whitney's bail hearing, Ian thinks of a way of helping Sharon and Shelley gives Linda a few home truths. Chantelle is having trouble juggling work and home life, so Mitch offers to step in and help. Ben hatches a plan to stay in control ahead of his meeting with Danny - but things turn sour when Callum arrives(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In March 2015, football fan Simon Dobbin was at an away game when he was set upon by a gang of men in an unprovoked attack that left him brain damaged, paralysed and unable to walk and talk. After many months in hospital he is finally back at home in Mildenhall Suffolk, where he receives round-the-clock care from wife Nicole. But the family home is unfit for Simon's care and rehabilitation - so that's where Nick Knowles, designer Laurence Llewelyn- Bowen and the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Prime Minister Folami declares a public holiday for the annual midsummer festival, to promote integration and quell the unrest - so Kamal plots to cause maximum chaos by ordering the Mercy Point cadets to stand patrol during the celebrations, hoping their presence will anger the festival-goers. Lekan makes it very clear to Callum that he wants him out - he is just waiting for the right moment - while the Hadleys cannot understand why Sephy is acting so differently, only for things to take a nasty turn when Meggie interferes in her friends' personal business(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Weston-super-Mare, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley goes out and about the south-east of England monitoring how police officers are taking on the crooks. She talks to fire officers who are appealing to the public to 'phone rather than film' when witnessing a fire. Back in the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest crime appeals from all over the UK(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period one-bedroom cottage near Blackwell in Worcestershire, a huge Methodist chapel in Dover in Kent and a three-bedroom end-of-terrace in Bootle on Merseyside are all sold to new, ambitious owners. Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality(n)

programme.title: Dom Does America
programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A poorly planned attempt at a frozen fraud melts away when investigators turn up the heat, while a prisoner claiming for injury behind bars forgets that the camera never lies. Presented by Naga Munchetty(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: David Harper and Gary Pe help two teams look for items of value in Liverpool's St Georges Hall. Host Eric Knowles crosses the Mersey to find out about the world's finest collection of Della Robbia pottery(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Valerie go to great lengths to exonerate Jimmi, only for him to be betrayed by a friend on the inside(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in south-west England to help a creative couple with a £805,000 budget escape their high-pressure jobs and buy a house on the Jurassic Coast from where they can run a luxury camping business. While in the area, Jonnie visits Britain's only thatched brewery which has been making ales for over 200 years, and learns from a family descendent how traditional brewing methods are still very much in use today(n)

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: At mainland Wales' most northerly point in Flintshire, Derek Brockway's latest walk starts off at the site of the Point of Ayr Colliery at the mouth of the Dee estuary. There, Derek meets John Wiltshire, who is passionate about keeping the memory of the mine alive. Derek lends a hand, helping endangered species in the sand dunes, and little terns who nest on the beach just over the Denbighshire border. Around 10 miles on, in 'Sunny Rhyl', Derek learns to kitesurf and do the surfers' shaka(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter Q, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore unearths three hidden gems that she believes have profit-making potential. She calls on lighting expert Guy Trench to find a new use for a collection of auction cast-offs, while designer Daniel Heath has a tallboy chest of drawers to get creative with. A flatpack dining set is given the upcycling treatment by Sarah herself(n)

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Colleagues Dean and Julian are paired with chefs Anna Haugh and Ellis Barrie, with fish likely to be on the menu as both work for a charity supporting fishing communities. Rylan Clark-Neal hosts(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Kym Marsh round off the week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste examine criminals extorting money from unsuspecting victims from online dating scams, beginning with a man who tricked a businesswoman out of a total of almost £60,000 over the course of a few months. As they unravel the evidence that allowed the fraudster to appear so plausible, a dramatic showdown with the conman awaits. Plus, a man reveals what it's like to have personal photos stolen and used as part of an online 'catfishing' campaign, and an extraordinary international con is exposed(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic hockey gold medallist Alex Danson, former Premier League footballer Matt Holland, Great Britain 400m great Roger Black and ex-England cricketer Isa Guha join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: After learning of Ben's arrest, Callum is distraught when Kathy reveals Ben was leaving the Prince Albert with another man and makes a difficult decision. Linda is unnerved when Shelley arrives at the Vic and tells Isaac about the landlady's drinking. Tiffany seeks advice from Callum over her problems with Keegan(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Restaurant critic Tom Parker Bowles sets the brief for the penultimate quarter-final, challenging the hopefuls to serve up a dish featuring chilli. John Torode and Gregg Wallace then decide who will be progressing further in the competition and who will be hanging up their aprons(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Actress and writer Daisy May Cooper, quiz guru Richard Osman, actress, comedian and writer Diane Morgan, and journalist and broadcaster Rory Reid join host Rob Brydon and regular captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy panel show. As ever, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory(n)

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian is not known for his culinary abilities, with most of his meals prepared in the microwave. So when he is challenged to cater for the punters at a busy festival, he does not have long to learn how to come up with some fusion food that he will be able to prepare in the confines of a small tent(n)

programme.title: Young, Welsh and Pretty Minted
programme.desc: Ibby presents his designs to professional golfer Andrew 'Beef' Johnston, while risque card boss Toni Leigh Hall has some life-changing news(n)

programme.title: Man Up (2015)
programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. However, she has to face the consequences when he realises she is not his intended blind date. Romantic comedy, starring Lake Bell and Simon Pegg(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.desc: Martin Roberts explores an ambitious housing development in Tredegar, Dion Dublin visits a house in Great Barr and Martel Maxwell is impressed by a six-bedroom terrace in Liverpool(n)

programme.desc: Dominic Littlewood samples life working as a game warden in Cameron County, Texas, where he joins warden Mike Gonzalez to learn about the challenges he faces in this role(n)

programme.desc: While traveling across London to attend a party to celebrate her parents' 10th wedding anniversary, a thirtysomething single woman is mistaken for a recently divorced stranger's blind date, so decides to spend a chaotic evening with him. programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms, and the memories they hold, back to life. The team set to work repairing a Jamaican pump organ, an RAF bomber pilot's hat, a naval rigging tool kit, and a crib made from wood salvaged from a church that was bombed during the First World War(n)

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: Another group of six amateur cooks prepare a Signature Dish for judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace, hoping to secure their place in the next stage of the contest. After two contestants are sent home, the remaining four take part in The Three Ingredients Challenge, creating a dish using chicken, mushrooms and spinach(n)

programme.title: The Top Table
programme.desc: Young people come to the Top Table to face down our politicians in an audience debate show, presented by Stephen Nolan(n)

programme.title: Hometown: A Killing
programme.desc: Following his 2019 investigation into the death of Mohammed Yassar Yaqub, journalist Mobeen Azhar returns to his hometown of Huddersfield to face his critics, whilst uncovering a new story of turf wars, police informers and a surprising new front line on the war on drugs(n)

programme.title: Hometown: A Killing
programme.desc: Another tragic killing leads Mobeen Azhar to examine police tactics being used to deal with the new threat of extreme violence which is spilling out onto the streets. Mobeen's investigation reveals new information about Yassar Yaqub's 'rival' King Meggy, and uncovers a promising new front on the war on drugs(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Carolyn brings unwelcome news and a change of priorities. Eve is finally going to meet Anna, leaving her questioning the change of direction when she is closer than ever to finding Villanelle. Kenny has some revelations that make Eve question the true purpose of their entire mission. Villanelle is still in prison, now in solitary confinement and waiting for a rescue organised by Konstantin that never seems to come(n)

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads on a house-hunting mission in the Kent countryside with a strong-minded couple who have a £900,000 budget and completely different views on the style of the large family home they want to buy. While in the county, Nicki gains an insight into Kent's cricketing heritage by seeing the impressive craftsmanship involved in producing hand-made cricket bats(n)

programme.title: Money for Nothing

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The locals anxiously await the outcome of Whitney's bail hearing, Ian thinks of a way of helping Sharon and Shelley gives Linda a few home truths. Chantelle is having trouble juggling work and home life, so Mitch offers to step in and help. Ben hatches a plan to stay in control ahead of his meeting with Danny - but things turn sour when Callum arrives(n)

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: In March 2015, football fan Simon Dobbin was at an away game when he was set upon by a gang of men in an unprovoked attack that left him brain damaged, paralysed and unable to walk and talk. After many months in hospital he is finally back at home in Mildenhall Suffolk, where he receives round-the-clock care from wife Nicole. But the family home is unfit for Simon's care and rehabilitation - so that's where Nick Knowles, designer Laurence Llewelyn- Bowen and the DIY SOS team come in, strengthened by a number of local tradesmen(n)

programme.title: Noughts + Crosses
programme.desc: Prime Minister Folami declares a public holiday for the annual midsummer festival, to promote integration and quell the unrest - so Kamal plots to cause maximum chaos by ordering the Mercy Point cadets to stand patrol during the celebrations, hoping their presence will anger the festival-goers. Lekan makes it very clear to Callum that he wants him out - he is just waiting for the right moment - while the Hadleys cannot understand why Sephy is acting so differently, only for things to take a nasty turn when Meggie interferes in her friends' personal business(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve

Eve and Kenny confront Carolyn, but get a surprise that leads them deeper into the conspiracy - and into a final showdown from which nobody emerges unscathed. Conclusion of the first series of the spy thriller based on Luke Jennings' novella series and adapted for television by Phoebe Waller-Bridge (Fleabag). Starring Jodie Comer, Sandra Oh, Fiona Shaw and Sean Delaney(n)

Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow progress as they get underway to make their property dreams a reality(n)

programme.desc: Dom helps track down fugitives with a bounty on their heads as he works as a private investigator in Waco, Texas. His mentor is James Fullner, a private investigator who's spent 15 years in the business, exposing cheating partners and business fraud, and apprehending fugitives on the run. Working alongside an armed team of investigators, on behalf of a local bail bondsman, they hit the streets in pursuit of a violent fugitive, a runaway drug addict and a wanted sex offender(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich are called in by a young family in Fishponds, Bristol, to create a garden to unwind in after work. Zoe and Jonny are dedicated charity workers who want and need a sanctuary spot to relax away from their emotionally challenging work. They also want the designers to find a way of including a place for Zoe to grow her wedding bouquet for their upcoming marriage, somewhere for new baby, Ottilie, to play and even an outdoor spot for Zoe to practise alfresco yoga(n)

programme.desc: Across the UK the number of people using of buses has plummeted in recent years. Outside London, thousands of routes have been cut and bus pass use is down. The Prime Minister has pledged billions to revitalise the bus network, but is it enough? Richard Bilton travels coast to coast across the north of England to see the reality of Britain's battered bus network. programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The UK is on the brink of a coronavirus epidemic and, with over-65s deemed particularly at risk, Geraint Vincent asks how the general public can protect the elderly from it. The older you get, the greater the chances that the virus could be fatal. Tonight explores why this is and which underlying conditions make people especially vulnerable. With the help of doctors and scientists, Geraint provides specialist advice on what to do if you or a relative has symptoms, tips on how to protect each other from infection, and what to do if you are elderly and told to self-isolate. How are hospitals preparing for what may now be inevitable rises in elderly admissions, and can an already-stretched care system cope? Should we avoid contact with our elderly relatives or should we carry on as normal? With the latest figures, top experts and government advice, the film provides the ultimate guide on how we can protect the elderly from this fast-moving virus(n)

programme.title: The Station: Trouble on the Tracks

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The UK is on the brink of a coronavirus epidemic and, with over-65s deemed particularly at risk, Geraint Vincent asks how the general public can protect the elderly from it. The older you get, the greater the chances that the virus could be fatal. Tonight explores why this is and which underlying conditions make people especially vulnerable. With the help of doctors and scientists, Geraint provides specialist advice on what to do if you or a relative has symptoms, tips on how to protect each other from infection, and what to do if you are elderly and told to self-isolate. How are hospitals preparing for what may now be inevitable rises in elderly admissions, and can an already-stretched care system cope? Should we avoid contact with our elderly relatives or should we carry on as normal? With the latest figures, top experts and government advice, the film provides the ultimate guide on how we can protect the elderly from this fast-moving virus(n)

programme.desc: Zoe and Emma discover some graffiti daubed on their house. Robert returns to school after his ordeal. Alan bans Eric from the pub. Tina warns Butch not to take revenge on Luke(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Intrigue spreads among the residents of Aidensfield as Frances Elliot causes havoc with her car. An outraged Peggy decides to investigate the rumours about the newcomer and discovers she appears to be hiding something. Rob receives some good news in the post but is later forced into making a life-changing decision. Kerrie Taylor guests(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin's hopes for a night of romance are dashed when Chrissy decides to supervise his girlfriend's visit. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy needs a partner for the annual staff dance, so Robin takes lessons from Mrs Roper. Flat-sharing comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan, Paula Wilcox and Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Attempts to scare off Robert's other mother leave him little to crow about(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. When young recruit PC Joe Mason (Joe McFadden) arrives he is not quite what they expected, with his tactics for crime fighting making his reception a mixed one between both residents and fellow police officers(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kathy seriously considers Chris's offer. Nellie disowns Tina and warns Zak and Butch to ignore her. The police tell Jack and Sarah that Simpson will not be prosecuted(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Derek Simpson startles Sarah when he turns up at the farm. Butch arrives at the McAllisters in a drunken state. Seth and Vic have a drinking competition(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve is aghast when his credit card is cut off. Curly hates being back at work under Reg. Des tells Sean he will not sack Liz as she is a good worker(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve tells Fiona about the extent of his debts. Liz is amazed when Sean apologises to her. Mavis is thrilled when Derek tells her he has decided to drop the compensation claim(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When director Nick Cheyney is found beheaded by a guillotine on the set of his latest film, Barnaby and Jones are left with no shortage of suspects. The victim, it transpires, was on poor terms with several people in some way connected to the project, including the mother of his child, and an actor who blamed him for ruining his career. Guest starring Nicky Henson(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A gang of bikers wreaks havoc in Aidensfield, prompting the locals to demand a new bobby. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Troubled saleswoman Helen (Nicola Walker), whose mother was murdered back in episode one, turns up on Janet's doorstep with information that takes the investigation in a completely different direction - paving the way for the formidable Det Supt Julie Dodson to be called in. Meanwhile, new recruit Sgt Rob Waddington arrives on the scene - putting Janet's nose out of joint as she is demoted back down to constable. Emmerdale star Danny Miller (Aaron Livesy) joins the cast(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack tackles what promises to be the most sensitive and unenviable investigation of his entire career - deciding whether a man with Down's syndrome is capable of committing murder. David Jason stars as the caustic copper, with Bruce Alexander and Matthew Marsh(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy discovers she has an admirer, while Mr Roper thinks his wife is seeing someone else. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan and Brian Murphy(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A teenager arrives in the village asking for directions to Ashfordly Hall - and claims to be looking for her father. David decides to make a will, but becomes paranoid when one of the beneficiaries seems too eager to inherit, and a series of accidents befall him. Elsewhere, a new arrival at the Fossett farm is named after Joe. Guest starring Ophelia Lovibond(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jessica is convinced Butch is going to come back and attack her. Lady Weir asks to meet with Eric. Dave advises Kathy to take the settlement and free herself from the Tates(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff tries to convince Luke that Tina should get an abortion. Jessica is too frightened to stay in the house on her own. Zak goes to see Tina and begs her to come home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona assures Steve she loves him, but asks for time to think about their future. Vera is disgusted to see Don entertaining Josie. Norris is glad Derek is dropping the claim(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Matchmaker Vera invites Tricia and Curly for tea. Raquel is stunned when Bet asks her to manage the pub in her absence. Mavis panics when Angela invites the Wiltons to dinner(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a family glassware business collapses following the death of one of its partners, Barnaby investigates allegations of financial irregularities, love triangles and a string of murders. Lapsed Freemason Jones uses his inside knowledge to explore the ceremonial aspects of two killings, but it is Cully's production of Hamlet that gives Tom the key to the mystery. John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Jane Wymark and Laura Howard star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A teenager arrives in the village asking for directions to Ashfordly Hall - and claims to be looking for her father. David decides to make a will, but becomes paranoid when one of the beneficiaries seems too eager to inherit, and a series of accidents befall him. Elsewhere, a new arrival at the Fossett farm is named after Joe. Guest starring Ophelia Lovibond(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A murder gives rise to rumours of a communist conspiracy. Poirot becomes the centre of attention at the Chelsea Flower Show as he views a rose named in his honour, while Hastings receives a packet of seeds that give him hay fever. Whodunit, guest starring Anne Stallybrass (Heartbeat), with David Suchet(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Having realised her marriage is a mistake, Rachel is still staying at Janet's and making excuses for her absence, but Sean isn't prepared to accept that things have gone so wrong, so quickly. Meanwhile, the investigation into Joe Bevan continues as the body count rises and the team faces the mammoth task of identifying the victims and determining the order in which they were murdered. Detective drama, starring Suranne Jones, Lesley Sharp and Sean Maguire(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Gruff detective Jack Frost's plans for a quiet weekend are disrupted by the gruesome discovery of an unidentified body floating in the river. However, soon after he has begun the investigation, he is forced to apply his deductive skills to a separate case involving an act of violence during a football match which left a player in a coma. Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy takes over Robin's poker game when he is rushed into hospital. Unfortunately, she does not fully understand the rules and puts the rent money at risk. Comedy, with Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When armed robbers hold up the night mail train, Joe is forced to work with old adversary DI Ludlow (David Calder) to catch the perpetrators. However, Ludlow's unwillingness to include the local police in the investigation leads Joe to believe he may be hiding something. Elsewhere, Peggy is excited to learn she has been left a legacy by a relation of her late husband. Eric Sykes makes a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak warns Butch to keep away from the McAllisters. Kathy taunts Chris about how much she enjoys being with Dave. Luke has a shock proposal for Tina(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina shows off her engagement ring, and Butch finds out that she is planning to marry Luke. Kathy agrees to take the settlement, which angers Kim(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet and Vicky go off on the cruise. Don is alarmed to find Josie in his house cleaning up. Norris seeks Derek's advice when Angela makes a play for him(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen wonders what Reg is up to when a mystery man keeps phoning. Relief manager Rodney Bostock arrives at the Rovers, and Jack tries to talk him into having a lock-in(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When armed robbers hold up the night mail train, Joe is forced to work with old adversary DI Ludlow (David Calder) to catch the perpetrators. However, Ludlow's unwillingness to include the local police in the investigation leads Joe to believe he may be hiding something. Elsewhere, Peggy is excited to learn she has been left a legacy by a relation of her late husband. Eric Sykes makes a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The acerbic GP investigates after local farmer Dennis Thorpe shoots at Robert for taking some of his eggs. Meanwhile, Emily returns to the surgery to see Martin after her acne medication caused a bad reaction, a secret bidder buys the Crab and Lobster pub, and Louisa ponders if she should see a fertility specialist. Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who is forced to retrain as a GP and takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village where he soon finds himself at odds with the locals(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa have their first fertility consultation with Dr Emma Ryder. Meanwhile, a case of vandalism at a local surf school is reported and PC Joe Penhale investigates. Elsewhere, Dr Edward Mullen returns to the surgery, and Bert is feeling the pressure from Caitlin. Starring Martin Clunes, Lucy Russell and Conleth Hill(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Dorothy becomes concerned her grand-daughter Taisie is being influenced by Rachel, while Janet is mortified when Gill berates her for inappropriate behaviour toward young sergeant Rob. The detective duo are sent to investigate the death of a man in a nursing home after his daughter complains about the level of care he had been receiving. Will they find any evidence of foul play? Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Danny Miller(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
