Crime drama, starring David Jason, Larry Lamb, Bruce Alexander, Lindy Whiteford and Berwick Kaler(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona assures Steve she loves him, but asks for time to think about their future. Vera is disgusted to see Don entertaining Josie. Norris is glad Derek is dropping the claim(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Matchmaker Vera invites Tricia and Curly for tea. Raquel is stunned when Bet asks her to manage the pub in her absence. Mavis panics when Angela invites the Wiltons to dinner(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jessica is convinced Butch is going to come back and attack her. Lady Weir asks to meet with Eric. Dave advises Kathy to take the settlement and free herself from the Tates(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff tries to convince Luke that Tina should get an abortion. Jessica is too frightened to stay in the house on her own. Zak goes to see Tina and begs her to come home(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A teenager arrives in the village asking for directions to Ashfordly Hall - and claims to be looking for her father. David decides to make a will, but becomes paranoid when one of the beneficiaries seems too eager to inherit, and a series of accidents befall him. Elsewhere, a new arrival at the Fossett farm is named after Joe. Guest starring Ophelia Lovibond(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Mr Roper plans to convert the flats into smaller dwelling units - but he has not bargained for Robin, Chrissy and Jo having a three-year lease. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy takes over Robin's poker game when he is rushed into hospital. Unfortunately, she does not fully understand the rules and puts the rent money at risk. Comedy, with Paula Wilcox and Richard O'Sullivan(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When armed robbers hold up the night mail train, Joe is forced to work with old adversary DI Ludlow (David Calder) to catch the perpetrators. However, Ludlow's unwillingness to include the local police in the investigation leads Joe to believe he may be hiding something. Elsewhere, Peggy is excited to learn she has been left a legacy by a relation of her late husband. Eric Sykes makes a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak warns Butch to keep away from the McAllisters. Kathy taunts Chris about how much she enjoys being with Dave. Luke has a shock proposal for Tina(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Tina shows off her engagement ring, and Butch finds out that she is planning to marry Luke. Kathy agrees to take the settlement, which angers Kim(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Bet and Vicky go off on the cruise. Don is alarmed to find Josie in his house cleaning up. Norris seeks Derek's advice when Angela makes a play for him(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maureen wonders what Reg is up to when a mystery man keeps phoning. Relief manager Rodney Bostock arrives at the Rovers, and Jack tries to talk him into having a lock-in(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby is delighted that his heroes Hired Gun are reuniting to play at the Midsomer Rocks festival - minus their drummer, who disappeared 30 years ago after driving his car into a quarry. However, a string of bizarre warnings convinces the paranoid lead singer that someone is trying to stop the reunion, and his fears are confirmed when the backing singer drops dead on stage. Radio 2 host Suzi Quatro guest stars(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: When armed robbers hold up the night mail train, Joe is forced to work with old adversary DI Ludlow (David Calder) to catch the perpetrators. However, Ludlow's unwillingness to include the local police in the investigation leads Joe to believe he may be hiding something. Elsewhere, Peggy is excited to learn she has been left a legacy by a relation of her late husband. Eric Sykes makes a guest appearance(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A prominent bank manager is almost killed by a hit-and-run driver just before he is due to set sail on the luxury liner Queen Mary. Convinced his life is in danger, he persuades Poirot to accompany him - and his valuable shipment of liberty bonds - to New York. But the Belgian sleuth's main concern is his own terrible sea-sickness. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The acerbic GP investigates after local farmer Dennis Thorpe shoots at Robert for taking some of his eggs. Meanwhile, Emily returns to the surgery to see Martin after her acne medication caused a bad reaction, a secret bidder buys the Crab and Lobster pub, and Louisa ponders if she should see a fertility specialist. Drama series, starring Martin Clunes as a surgeon who is forced to retrain as a GP and takes up a post in a sleepy Cornish village where he soon finds himself at odds with the locals(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Martin and Louisa have their first fertility consultation with Dr Emma Ryder. Meanwhile, a case of vandalism at a local surf school is reported and PC Joe Penhale investigates. Elsewhere, Dr Edward Mullen returns to the surgery, and Bert is feeling the pressure from Caitlin. Starring Martin Clunes, Lucy Russell and Conleth Hill(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Dorothy becomes concerned her grand-daughter Taisie is being influenced by Rachel, while Janet is mortified when Gill berates her for inappropriate behaviour toward young sergeant Rob. The detective duo are sent to investigate the death of a man in a nursing home after his daughter complains about the level of care he had been receiving. Will they find any evidence of foul play? Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Danny Miller(n)

programme.title: World Cup Rivalries
programme.desc: Revisiting some of the most riveting matches played by the rival footballing nations(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as another four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The medical examiner performs an autopsy when a man demands answers over the death of his brother, who died following an operation by an eminent surgeon(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Anonymous messages begin turning up informing a model that someone close to her is in danger of being murdered - and which leave her doubting her own sanity(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A criminal escapes from Chelmsford prison protesting his innocence and hoping to get the media's help to secure a release date - so Arthur tries to make money by selling his exclusive story to the highest bidder in Fleet Street. George Cole and John Labanowski star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 uncovers a plan to hold the home secretary hostage in order to demand the release of a dangerous prisoner. Cowley takes the politician's place at an award ceremony, hoping that with Bodie as his bodyguard, he can draw the enemy into the open and thwart the attempt. Drama, starring Martin Shaw, Gordon Jackson and Lewis Collins, and featuring Pamela Stephenson in an early role(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The Flying Squad is called in to catch a gunman who has stolen cash from a lock-up. Hard-hitting detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.desc: Coverage of day two from Venue Cymru in Llandudno, featuring the conclusion of two quarter-finals, played over the best of 17 frames. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)

programme.title: The Professionals

programme.title: Snooker: Tour Championship Live
programme.desc: Further coverage of day two from Venue Cymru in Llandudno, featuring the conclusion of two quarter-finals, played over the best of 17 frames. Jill Douglas presents, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus and Neal Foulds, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates(n)

programme.title: Inside

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: One of the biggest events on the NYMR timetable is the annual War Weekend. The event, which sees the whole railway transformed into a nostalgic snapshot of wartime Britain, is hugely popular with steam enthusiasts, war re-enactors and others fascinated by days gone by. Coming towards the end of the railway's season the event is vital to bring in much-needed funds to see it through the quiet winter months. For its 20th year, the railway has brought in a new attraction - a huge Second World War artillery gun. However, getting the monster gun into position proves a mission in itself(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Britain by Barge: Then & Now
programme.desc: Bill Oddie, Jennie Bond, Anne Diamond and Pete Waterman learn how canals evolved into places of leisure. Pete revisit one of his childhood haunts, Braunston Marina, to meet the owner who turned around its fortunes, while Jennie discovers canal camping, an activity holiday popular with children since the 1960s. The foursome head off from Braunston taking the Oxford canal towards Banbury, discovering that the Oxford is a very different canal to those experienced earlier. Built by James Brindley to be as cheap as possible, it slowly carries them south through farmland and past sleepy villages(n)

programme.title: When TV Double Acts Fall Out

programme.title: Tulsa (1949)

programme.title: The Odessa File (1974)
programme.desc: The detailed diaries of a former inmate of a concentration camp fall into the hands of an investigative journalist. Incensed by what he reads, he embarks on a crusade to discover the prison commandant's new identity and expose his crimes to the world. In the process, the trail leads him to a secret organisation that helped Nazi SS officers escape justice. Thriller, based on Frederick Forsyth's novel, starring Jon Voight, Derek Jacobi and siblings Maria and Maximilian Schell(n)

programme.title: The Inn of the Sixth Happiness (1958)
programme.desc: English servant Gladys Aylward fulfills her dream of becoming a missionary in China. Working from a roadside inn used by mule caravans, she ends up caring for dozens of orphans, who she courageously leads to safety through hostile and mountainous territory during the Sino-Japanese War. Fact-based drama, starring Ingrid Bergman, with Curt Jurgens, Ronald Squire and Robert Donat(n)

programme.title: Crocodile Dundee II (1988)

Having rescued her from their clutches, he realises they will soon be coming after him - so he decides to even the odds by luring them to the Outback for a final showdown. Comedy adventure sequel, starring Paul Hogan, Linda Kozlowski, John Meillon and Charles S Dutton(n)

programme.title: The Wolverine (2013)

He ends up robbed of his superhuman powers by a mysterious scientist and is forced to protect the tycoon's granddaughter from a secret conspiracy's assassins. Superhero adventure spin-off, starring Hugh Jackman and Tao Okamoto(n)

programme.title: Baywatch (2017)
programme.desc: A veteran lifeguard commands a highly trained team that keeps the sun-kissed resort of Emerald Bay safe. As he breaks in some new recruits - including an insubordinate former Olympic swimmer - he has to contend with a dangerous new drug sweeping the beach. Action comedy based on the TV series, starring Dwayne Johnson, Zac Efron, Alexandra Daddario and Priyanka Chopra(n)

programme.title: Rudderless (2014)

Macy, starring Billy Crudup, Anton Yelchin and Felicity Huffman(n)

programme.title: Man in the Saddle (1951)
programme.desc: A wealthy and ambitious cattleman plans to seize all the land surrounding his own. A peaceful rancher living nearby ignores warnings to sell up and avoid a conflict, and as he suffers a campaign of intimidation, he is forced to adopt violent methods to defend his livelihood. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Alexander Knox, Joan Leslie and Ellen Drew(n)

programme.title: The Fighting Kentuckian (1949)
programme.desc: French settlers are granted asylum in America in the aftermath of Napoleon's defeat at Waterloo and a Kentucky rifleman finds himself falling for one of the newcomers - a general's daughter. However, he faces a rival for her affections in the form of a ruthless landowner who plans to steal the terrritory her fellow exiles have settled on. George Waggner's Western, starring John Wayne, Vera Hruba Ralston, Philip Dorn and Oliver Hardy(n)

programme.title: The Guns of Fort Petticoat (1957)
programme.desc: A lieutenant deserts after his commanding officer launches an unprovoked attack on a Comanche village during the Civil War. Realising that there are no soldiers in his home town to protect it from reprisals, he returns to train the local women to defend themselves. Western, starring Audie Murphy, Kathryn Grant, Hope Emerson and Jeff Donnell(n)

programme.title: Juggernaut (1974)
programme.desc: A transatlantic liner and its 1,200 passengers are threatened by a bomber, forcing a team of demolition experts into a desperate race against time to avert a catastrophe. Thriller, starring Richard Harris, Anthony Hopkins, Omar Sharif, Ian Holm and David Hemmings(n)

programme.title: Pitch Perfect 2 (2015)

programme.title: Stand Up Guys (2012)

They set out to recapture their glory days with one last burst of criminal activity, but one of the trio is under pressure from a Mob boss and has a dangerous secret that threatens them all. Crime comedy, starring Al Pacino, Christopher Walken and Alan Arkin(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: The team visits Swindon and Chippenham, where Henry Cole finds and restores a much-maligned BSA Ariel 3 moped and Simon O'Brien gives a Ford Transit gearbox a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Couple Mariam and Johnno arrive at their new chateau and discover they have no running water, and Julia has a decision to make when she re-visits a chateaux she fell in love with(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Trecarne House in Liskeard, Cornwall, where owners Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan hope to demonstrate that their B&B offers the best value for money. The pair win the group's admiration by sharing their life story, but one guest stirs up intrigue by holding his cards close to his chest(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners spend their second night of the competition at the Magna Carta at Windsor in Berkshire, a five-star floating bed and breakfast run by Captain Dominic Read. The guests are pleasantly surprised upon arrival, but not all are impressed by the standard of the accommodation - and a cracked teapot at the breakfast table leads to trouble for the proprietor(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third B&B of the week is Ash Cottage near Leeds Castle in Kent, where the unusual decor is an eye-opener for the guests but is not everyone's cup of tea. Trish and Tom get heated over the instant coffee before owners Rayne and Chris Deal take their fellow contestants to Chatham Dockyard to try their hands at rope-making(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final guest house of the week is A Room with a View in Brighton, where owner Stephen Bull and housekeeper Joanna Harris hope their nine-room establishment will impress the contestants. Things get off to a good start when Rayne and Chris Deal are impressed by the cleanliness of the property, but things take a turn for the worse when breakfast gets the thumbs down(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners come together to confront their critics face-to-face. Hosts of Trecarne House in Cornwall Trish and Tom Brock-Morgan raise issues with Magna Carta's Dominic Read on his mark for their hosting, while he has a bone to pick with them over criticism of his five-star floating B&B. Stephen and Joanna of A Room with a View in Brighton receive complaints about their breakfast, and tensions run high as the payments are revealed and the winner is announced(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The Hi Way elephants have been hit with a potentially deadly virus, with the youngest members of the family most at risk, and the keepers and Chester Zoo's veterinary team do all they can to combat the mysterious illness. Meanwhile, Mac the stallion zebra is introduced to the zoo's three mares in the hope they will produce much needed offspring for this endangered species. There's also a look into the bizarre world of the naked mole rat(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw restore a Renault 5 GT Turbo belonging to a man who was forced to abandon work on it after suffering serious organ failure(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul host an artists' retreat, and while she steps in as a model, he struggles in the kitchen. Angel gives Abbie and Karen a few tips for their wedding business(n)

programme.title: Jon Snow's Very Hard Questions
programme.desc: Singers on Buzzers and Shrewd Operators go head to head as they face tough questions on topics including science, sport and history. Hosted by Jon Snow(n)

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton is in Yorkshire with Helen and her husband Gary, who have grand plans for a huge self-build on the outskirts of historic York(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Peter Maddison meets a couple in the bush ranger territory of the Strathbogie Ranges, who are building a new home with magnificent views(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The team treats 78-year-old Kevin, who suffered a cardiac arrest at the gym and fortunately received life-saving treatment at the scene from an instructor trained to use a portable defibrillator. Meanwhile, 23-year-old paramedic Declan is sent for an emergency CT scan, and Monica, 83, is experiencing numbness in her leg following a fall at home, and her initial X-ray appears to indicate she has a broken neck(n)

programme.desc: The team revisits favourite investigations, including Kate Quilton's visit to the world's largest producer of jelly beans to find out what goes into the sweets. Matt Tebbutt attempts to find out why tinned anchovies taste so salty, and Jimmy Doherty heads to a Mauritius distillery as he explores the secrets of rum(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Vintage Gems
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp teams up with potter Richard Miller to help Ellie and Nick Harrington - who have no practical DIY skills - create plates to complement their 1930s-style dining room in Tunbridge Wells, Kent(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman shows a range of properties to Marc and Mat, who are looking to buy a holiday retreat on Majorca on a budget of £200,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps teenage sweethearts Terry and Kay find a property in the Costa Almeria area of southern Spain on a budget of £100,000(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole makes good money restoring an antique pine sideboard in Sheffield, but has less success with a pair of old brass jugs, while Simon converts a staircase into shelves(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Hampshire and Surrey, where Henry makes a pair of coffee tables from a cable drum and a galvanised water tank, and Simon rescues a winged back chair and upholsters it with his own designer fabric(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul host an artists' retreat, and while she steps in as a model, he struggles in the kitchen. Angel gives Abbie and Karen a few tips for their wedding business(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is Nateby Inn in Cumbria, where landlady Gaynor Walker is ready with a warm welcome. A trip to Gaynor's local brewery turns out to be a barrel of laughs when one guest beats another in a cask-rolling competition, before a broken cooker creates chaos in the kitchen the following morning(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners visit the Outfields Farm Bed and Breakfast in Ravensden, Bedfordshire, on their second stay, where proprietors Graham and Julia Ashley treat them to an afternoon of indoor sky-diving before hosting a dinner filled with surprises. The guests enjoy a good night's sleep, but one of the owners is expecting hassle at breakfast time(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to At Home in Chiswick, west London, where artist Bob Osbourne appears to take a rather laid-back approach to running his B&B. On arrival, guests are pleasantly surprised by their first impressions of the communal living space-come-mini art gallery - but it's not long before they are dishing the dirt(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition draws to a close at the Ammonite Lodge guesthouse in Chard, Somerset, where owners Andrew and Clare Ashmore hope to excite their guests with a game of skittles. During the final dinner of the week, the competitors discuss their first impressions of each other, and Bob and Julia share some amusing observations with the group(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners confront their critics face to face, and the winners are announced. Nateby Inn host Gaynor takes issue with Graham and Julia's reduced marks for cobwebs. The conversation becomes heated when Outfields Farm's owners tackle Gaynor's complaints in return, and Georgie jumps to their defence. Artist Bob Osborne has a long list of grievances he wants to address, and Julia is left cringing when her previous comments rebound on her(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Cameras follow Chester Zoo's four new fledgling Humboldt penguins who are being brought up apart from the main colony until they're old enough to compete for fish in the main pool. They're keen to make a break for it and join the adults, but when they're finally free to go they have other ideas. Elsewhere, Niall the chimpanzee keeper has known chimp Boris for 44 years. When the ape captures a moorhen chick, Niall must use his powers of persuasion to negotiate the safe release of his hostage(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend renovate more classic cars, beginning by restoring a 1967 Fiat 500 for a man who suffered a series of family tragedies(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Emma and Paul plan to host locals - including the mayor - to view the work they have done on the front of their chateau. However, Paul has first to design, build and, with a bit of advice from Dick Strawbridge, fit two heavy balconies from scratch. Elsewhere, Mariam and Johnno get their antique fireplace fitted, create a stylish sink out of a copper bucket, and host a 'thank you' meal for their builders(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Spanish architect Jaime and his wife Mimi as they embark on a mission to convert a Grade II listed folly into a family home in Buckinghamshire. Giving themselves a wildly optimistic six-month deadline to finish before the birth of their second child, it's not long before the couple hit trouble. Jaime puts all his energies and creativity into the project, using innovative 3D mapping to squeeze living spaces into the tower, but it proves to be an exhausting struggle(n)

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: Daily life on the mammoth cruise ship the Symphony of the Seas, which boasts 20 pools and jacuzzis, two surf simulators, a rock-climbing wall, an ice rink, a basketball court and a mini golf course. It's all hands on deck for the crew members as 6,680 passengers arrive for the holiday of a lifetime(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Luxurious Airline
programme.desc: While leading airlines battling it out for the number one spot as the world's most luxurious flyer, this documentary reveals the exacting standards first-class passengers expect when wining and dining in the skies - with Michelin-style food, limitless champagne, and a deluxe first-class suite for the super wealthy. With exclusive access to Singapore Airlines, the film reveals the lengths the airline goes to building a first-class suite for the super wealthy, spending $850m over the course of four years(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Cameras focus on the five per cent of the UK's families who are collectively responsible for a staggering amount of the nation's crime - 50 per cent of criminal arrests. Police are dispatched to apprehend a drunk young man outside a nightclub. Some members of his family have amassed 193 arrests and 111 convictions between them(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr invites Sean Lock and Kevin Bridges to take on guest captain Joe Wilkinson and Victoria Coren Mitchell in the words-and-numbers quiz. Special guest Adam Kay joins wordsmith Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers(n)

programme.title: Billion Pound Cruise
programme.desc: Daily life on the mammoth cruise ship the Symphony of the Seas, which boasts 20 pools and jacuzzis, two surf simulators, a rock-climbing wall, an ice rink, a basketball court and a mini golf course. It's all hands on deck for the crew members as 6,680 passengers arrive for the holiday of a lifetime(n)

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: Cameras focus on the five per cent of the UK's families who are collectively responsible for a staggering amount of the nation's crime - 50 per cent of criminal arrests. Police are dispatched to apprehend a drunk young man outside a nightclub. Some members of his family have amassed 193 arrests and 111 convictions between them(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: A selection of previous investigations from the programme delving into the secrets behind mass-produced food. Jimmy Doherty heads to Kenya to find out what makes one tea more expensive than another, before meeting a taster for one of the UK's largest tea producers. Matt Tebbutt braves the icy waters of the North Sea to join divers harvesting scallops by hand from the sea bed, while Kate Quilton is in Israel to learn why some limes are much juicier than others(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits Manhattan, where she creates candy canes and lollipops at artisan sugar crafter Papabubble, before heading back to the UK to learn how to make passion fruit marshmallows covered in chocolate and coconut(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman meets Bernie and George Wilks, who have sold their house in the UK and want to start a new adventure living in the Spanish area of Oliva, on a budget of £75,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jacqui Lockhart has worked hard all her life, and now that she has the savings available she wants to fulfil her lifelong dream of owning a holiday home on Spain's southern Costa Blanca. With a cash budget of £50,000 and the support of her daughter Judy, the pressure is on for Danni Menzies to help Jacqui secure her dream property(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien head to Redditch where Henry restores a 1970s Kawasaki motorbike, while Simon takes a pair of rusty cinema seat supports and creates a desirable garden bench(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Wales, where Henry restores a Royal Enfield motorbike, converting it into a classic cafe racer. Simon's choice of an old school house radiator springs a leak, but he gets an 1980s pinball machine back to its best(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY

Elsewhere, Mariam and Johnno get their antique fireplace fitted, create a stylish sink out of a copper bucket, and host a 'thank you' meal for their builders(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Another selection of B&B owners take it in turns as hosts to decide which of their establishments provides the best value for money. The contest begins at Ian and Sam Sugden's Abbey House in Penrith, Cumbria, where the guests are impressed with the cleanliness of the rooms. However, there is some grumbling over breakfast about the bacon(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners stay at Compton House in Newark, Nottinghamshire, which is run by Lisa Holloway - who prides herself on providing a home away from home for her visitors. However, some of the guests have issues with dust and the haddock served at breakfast - and challenge whether a night's stay is worth the price tag(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Dave and Jo Bate welcome their guests to the White Horse, a pub and B&B in Ripon, North Yorkshire, where they hope an afternoon of morris dancing in front of the cathedral will make a lasting impression. Unfortunately, Ed and Jane Mitchell get locked inside their room by mistake, and there are arguments over tomatoes at breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Retired teachers Ed and Jane Mitchell welcome the contestants to the Old Farmhouse, Cheshire, but everyone is shocked to discover they will have to share a bathroom. Meanwhile, Sam and Ian Sugden find an unwelcome surprise underneath their bed(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Payment day arrives, as the guests gather to find out who has won and to thrash out their differences - from some fishy findings to a reminder of why sorry can be the hardest word. Later on, a heated exchange about cleaning ends in tears(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: With numbers in the wild dwindling rapidly, Chester Zoo's black rhino breeding programme is a key part of preserving the species. Black rhino Kitani is introduced to new mate Magadi, but with injury a high risk during mating, keeper Babs is concerned that Kitani may struggle to defend herself, since she lost her horn after the death of her calf. Meanwhile, there's a surprise birth in the orangutan house, and older sibling Tuti has to fend for herself and set out on the road to independence(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: The Sunbeam Alpine, a sleek two-seater convertible, was one of the most stylish British sports cars of the 1960s, and is now back in fashion and much sought after. Former mechanic and motor racing enthusiast Ray bought his Sunbeam Alpine over 20 years ago as a retirement project. Ray was always an active cyclist and skier, but after he suffered a brain injury six years ago he was left with severe disabilities. Knowing how much the car means to him, Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend use all their motoring know-how and parts-blagging guile to get Ray's car back on the road(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge steps in to help new chateau owners couple Erin and JB who, having recently moved into their fairy-tale chateau complete with turrets and a moat, have discovered rising damp. Dick goes on an exploration of their vast cellar in search of the leak(n)

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Harry and Briony Anscombe, who have decided to move from London to Cornwall with their three young children and begin building work on an ambitious house, inspired by American modernist properties. With a budget of just £400,000, Harry opts to project manage himself, paying local builders by the hour, but his lack of experience in construction proves to be problematic(n)

programme.title: Thou Shalt Not Kill
programme.desc: Valeria Ferro, the intuitive head of the Homicide unit in Turin, has a new romantic interest, but is still coming to terms with the fact Mario Ferro is not her biological father and news that her mother Lucia has disappeared. Amid her whirl of personal drama, a woman is found, strangled. The victim, Susanna Bianco, had fled to a domestic violence shelter, and her husband - involved in an accident near the shelter around the time of the murder - is the chief suspect. Miriam Leone stars. In Italian(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Four-year-old Alfie rushed in with a broken thighbone after falling off his bike, the medical team suspects 19-year-old rugby player Chris has suffered neck and spinal injuries, while James, 42, arrives with chest pains and breathing difficulties. Documentary following life inside one of Britain's busiest A&E departments at King's College Hospital in south London.(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary follows the work of the stroke team at south London's King's College Hospital. Laura's family faces a big decision when the 72-year-old grandmother is found to have a blood clot on her brain, while father-of-three Nic, the over-40s National BMX Champion, collapses in reception after suffering an accident while training for a stunt. The medics also treat 83-year-old Marjorie, who is complaining of a pain behind her eye following a recent bout of shingles(n)

programme.title: American Pickers

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon and Lori discover a dead cow in one of their storage unit lots(n)

programme.title: Storage Hunters
programme.desc: Brandon wins some spy equipment at an auction in Culver City(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike Wolfe and Frank Fritz have their work cut out for them on a huge property, which includes a collection of more custom 1932 Fords than they have ever seen in one place(n)

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: The guys get the pick of a lifetime at a famous tourist attraction, plus a collector in Cape Cod shuts down a deal until his wife steps in(n)

programme.title: Timber Kings
programme.desc: Andre is on cue to deliver a pool table, until a game changing request. Meanwhile, it is sink or swim for Joel and Bryan Sr as they build their first ever log boathouse(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to discover if they can go motor racing for less than it costs to play golf. There's also a double helping of guests, with rock star Slash and Formula One driver Kimi Raikkonen taking to the track in Reasonably Priced Cars. Richard also gets behind the wheel of the BMW M5, which is powered by a twin-turbo V8 engine instead of the high-revving V10 of its predecessor(n)

programme.title: Ultimate Movers
programme.desc: The team at King's Haulage deal with the UK's largest bronze sculpture when it berths at Plymouth Docks, while the race is on to deliver a herd of Lego dinosaurs to Marwell Zoo(n)

programme.title: Scrapyard Supercar
programme.desc: Pro driver Shane Lynch allows engineer Ralph Hosier and his teams four days in the scrapyard to build two vehicles that then go against Shane's Audi R8 V10 in a duel test of acceleration and braking(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May immerse themselves in nostalgia and try to prove that hot hatches from their younger days are better than today's equivalents. Jeremy grabs the keys to a Volkswagen Golf GTI, Richard takes the wheel of a Vauxhall Nova SRi and James May opts for the Ford Fiesta XR2i, and the trio set off on a road trip that includes a hill climb and a visit to a supermarket. However, their adventure concludes at an army training ground in Wales, where they and their steeds must face the heavily armed Top Gear Police Department. Downton Abbey star Hugh Bonneville blasts the Reasonably Priced Car around the test track(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. Meanwhile, James May spends time at Camp Bastion in Afghanistan to learn about the army's vehicle redeployment efforts, and Thor star Tom Hiddleston tries to coax the best time he can muster from the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: The comedians are challenged to taste-test some mystery baby food, Russell Howard presents a nightmarish fairy tale and Liza Tarbuck introduces a plastic lizard to a cordless drill(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner introduces a look back at some of the best bits from the series, plus a few hitherto unseen clips. Topics nominated for consignment to oblivion include dabbing, people who love the outdoors and The Archers(n)

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Impressionist and actor Alistair McGowan, former Dragons' Den businesswoman Hilary Devey and singer Josh Groban compete to have their pet hates banished, on the comedy panel show chaired by Frank Skinner. Their gripes include supermarkets, the smoking ban, Valentine's Day and people who treat animals like humans(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Extended version of a 2007 edition of the satirical current affairs quiz, presented by Kirsty Young. Former Labour politician Bob Marshall-Andrews and Dr Phil Hammond join regular team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond gets behind the wheel of the mid-engined Alfa Romeo 4C on the shores of Lake Como in Italy - but his enjoyment of the lightweight vehicle is disturbed by the arrival of Jeremy Clarkson, who demands a race against his quad bike. Jeremy also visits Belgium, putting the McLaren P1 hypercar to the test in Bruges and around the Spa-Francorchamps Formula One circuit - and its combined power output of more than 900bhp generated from electricity and turbocharged petrol V8 power is likely to cause astonishment. programme.title: Cop Car Workshop
programme.desc: John Heussi embarks on a complete redesign of Cheshire Constabulary's fleet of outdated forensic vans and a crime car comes in with a faulty wailer - but the fault remains elusive(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The three petrolheads demonstrate their love of small cars with a trip to Ukraine. Jeremy Clarkson chooses a Volkswagen Up for the journey, while Richard Hammond takes a Ford Fiesta and James May opts for a Dacia Sandero. On their adventure, the trio absorb the history of the Crimean Peninsula, including a visit to an old submarine pen - but the carefree exploration looks likely to become a chore when Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to a long drive north through the expansive country, during which they must combat serious boredom. And as if that wasn't arduous enough, a new task is set when they arrive in Kiev - a trip into the wasteland of Chernobyl. This edition also features a track test of the Danish Zenvo ST1 supercar and James Blunt returns for another drive in the Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson heads to Italy to get behind the wheel of the exclusive, hand-crafted Alfa Romeo Disco Volante, which is made by coachbuilder Touring Superleggera and based on the manufacturer's already rare 8C Competizione sports car. programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: Experts analyse the work of engineers including Sir Nigel Gresley, who engineered the world's fastest steam train in 1938 and Eric Laithwaite, whose linear motor made frictionless travel a possibility. These innovators influenced the engineering of the Shanghai Maglev, the fastest passenger train in operation on Earth, which broke numerous engineering boundaries in its construction. Held in place by a series of electromagnets, it levitates on an air gap of 10mm and is able to reach a top speed of 431kms per hour(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

programme.title: Operation Gold Rush with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Felicity Aston and Dr Kevin Fong finally arrive at the end of their gruelling journey in Dawson City. They set up a traditional mining camp in the heart of the gold fields, before facing their next challenge. They have just five days to find gold in an area that has already been heavily mined, and can only use the back-breaking methods of the 19th-century early pioneers(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Airbus A380, which as the largest passenger plane ever built can carry more than 850 people non-stop nearly half-way around the world. With the largest wingspan of any commercial aircraft and four specially developed mighty turbo jet engines, the A380 weighs 560 tonnes and getting it off the ground would not have been possible without the work of past innovators such as George Cayley and Frank Whittle. Key developments in aerospace engineering - from Richard Whitcombe's energy-efficient wing-tip designs to Nasa engineers' cutting-edge control system, Fly by Wire - helped bring the A380 to fruition(n)

programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. programme.title: How to Beat
programme.desc: Kate Quilton and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim explore small changes people can make to take care of their mental and physical health, beginning with methods of lowering the biological age. They recruit eight volunteers to take part in an experiment to see if simple lifestyle changes, such as walking quicker and getting a good night's sleep, can really turn back the clock. Kate also visits the English National Ballet to see how dance can benefit people with Parkinson's disease and Sam Renke explores how the fitness industry treats people with disabilities(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

Hosted by Jimmy Carr, with regular Countdown mathematician Rachel Riley looking after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: The Undateables
programme.desc: Following three new singletons on their search for love. Nineteen-year-old royalist Sam, who is autistic, has planned his own nuptials right down to the smallest details, despite never going on a date. 22-year-old creative Jodie, who loves making her own jewellery and designing her own clothes, longs to find a partner before she will need to rely on her wheelchair in later life. Meanwhile, 29-year-old hockey player Hannah, who has a functional neurological disorder following a random seizure, is looking for a special someone to share her life with(n)

programme.title: Five Guys a Week

programme.title: Location, Location, Location

Kirstie helps doting father Charlie, who is recently divorced, and his two children find a new family home as they start a new chapter in their lives. Elsewhere, Phil meets Ella and Grant, a couple who are longing for their own space as they want to start a family(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: A look back at Timothy West and Prunella Scales' travels in Sweden, where they finally fulfilled their lifetime ambitions of crossing the country coast-to-coast by boat. The 350-mile journey takes in the mighty Göta Canal, Sweden's three biggest lakes, the islands of the Swedish Archipelago, and the Baltic sea - a huge challenge for any sailor let alone two octogenarians more used to the Kennet and Avon Canal. Even the relaxation is testing - a Swedish sauna with an icy plunge into the waters of the canal, but the long tranquil summer days, island-dotted lakes, and woodland glades make it Tim's favourite-ever trip(n)

programme.desc: Kate Quilton and Dr Javid Abdelmoneim continue their health check of the UK, enlisting 10 stressed-out volunteers, ranging from a firefighter and a paramedic to a chef, to find out if simple lifestyle changes can alleviate stress. programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer go house-hunting in the Kentish countryside. Kirstie helps doting father Charlie, who is recently divorced, and his two children find a new family home as they start a new chapter in their lives. Elsewhere, Phil meets Ella and Grant, a couple who are longing for their own space as they want to start a family(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke(n)

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off for Stand Up to Cancer
programme.desc: Oscar winner Richard Dreyfuss, former EastEnder Patsy Palmer, The Inbetweeners star James Buckley and TV personality Scarlett Moffatt take part in the culinary challenge. Presenters Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding guide them through the assorted tasks, while Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith pass judgment on their baking skills(n)

programme.title: Gogglebox

programme.title: House

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The grumpy medic treats a 16-year-old girl who collapsed at her school's Christmas pageant. He determines her liver is failing, but cannot fathom why. Meanwhile, Foreman and Thirteen grow closer during the trials for the Huntington's disease drug. Hugh Laurie stars(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Letting agent Paul has a tenant in Lancashire who is behind with his rent and won't return any calls, but the situation is much worse than he could possibly have imagined when he heads into the upstairs bedroom. Harrow enforcement officers Andrew and Ozge are called to a local salon, where a leak from the upstairs flat is disrupting Masi's ability to run her shop. Once the landlord agrees to give them access, they discover terrible conditions and a back yard piled with rubbish(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Following a tip-off, Harrow Enforcement officers are shocked to discover an industrial-scale cannabis farm in a house. The team, along with the police, are determined to find out who has been running this illegal operation. After complaints of all-night parties in a quiet street in Darwen, Lancashire, letting agent Paul pays the revellers a visit. The house is in terrible shape, but the young tenants promise they will clear it all up and keep the noise down. Instead, however, the partying gets worse, with some of the tenants' 'associates' making nasty threats(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Officers chase £4,500 in court fees in north Wales after a property deal turned sour, and in Birmingham the agents try to collect over £3,500 from a single mum who defaulted on loan repayments. Elsewhere in Worcestershire, the enforcement agents try to recover more than £10,000 from a shop-keeper for fraudulently keeping a deposit during a property sale(n)

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Sheriffs Stewart and Elmor try to recover almost £2,400 from a company director for unpaid invoices in Manchester, but face a fierce reception from a family who deny the debtor lives or trades from the address. programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: House
programme.desc: The team treats an elderly woman who has been caring for her dying husband, but has been stricken with an unknown disease that threatens to kill her first. The doctors' diagnosis is made more difficult when one of their number suffers a personal tragedy. Guest starring Meat Loaf(n)

programme.title: House
programme.desc: Cameron puts her holiday with Chase on hold and pleads with House to examine an environmental campaigner who mysteriously collapsed during a protest. The doctor accepts, on condition that she does most of the testing on the patient. Hugh Laurie and Lisa Edelstein star(n)

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Jack Callahan arrives with Gabe for Mark and Paige's big day and Paul's attempt to matchmake between Jane and Des Clarke does not go to plan(n)

programme.title: 10 Years Younger in 10 Days
programme.desc: Cherry Healey comes to the aid of busy mum Myrah, who is desperate for a new image to help her succeed as a life coach. To help with her goal, Myrah gets a lesson in applying a slick make-up look. But can she be persuaded to replace her old-fashioned wardrobe? The experts also help mum-of-one Lyndsey, who wants to reinvent herself after battling cancer. A haircut and wardrobe are easy - but restoring her damaged teeth and skin might be a problem(n)

programme.title: Filthy House SOS
programme.desc: Cleaning duo Jon and Brennan help illusionist James in Dorset, who has neglected his career, his life, and his home following the recent loss of his parents(n)

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords

programme.title: The Sex Business
programme.desc: With access to an array of individuals working within Britain's multimillion-pound sex industry, this documentary takes a look at how people buy, sell and market sex. In the first edition, a disparate group of relatively new recruits to Britain's flourishing and fast-growing porn industry reveals what life is really like when trying to make a living in the adult movie industry(n)

programme.title: First Time Call Girl
programme.desc: Women from the world of escorting discuss what really happens behind closed doors, following 22-year-old Alexis as she journeys into the sex industry and explores the reasons behind her choices. The programme also follows Kat, who is considering leaving her escorting career behind, and 28-year-old Cassandra, a high end call girl who charges £3,000 per day(n)

programme.title: Shock Docs
programme.desc: Documentary about men obsessed with how they look and determined to turn themselves into the perfect specimen, either through the use of make-up or by going under the knife. They include 32-year-old Brazilian Rodrigo, who has gained worldwide notoriety for his plastic-doll-like body, and cosmetic virgin Junaid, who is under pressure on social media to have his nose fixed. On the flipside, Kieran is on a mission to remain 100 per cent natural, convinced that being beautiful start and ends with being self-confident. But will it bring him any luck with the ladies?(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Botched Up Bodies
programme.desc: Former boxing promoter Kellie Maloney was left fighting for her life after a second round of facial feminisation to make her face more womanly. Former model Linsey Dawn McKenzie managed to receive an unintended facelift, which left her with no earlobes and huge scars. Plus, the programme also follows La Toya Jackson's surgical journey(n)

programme.title: Sinkholes: Buried Underground
programme.desc: Documentary examining the underlying forces behind sinkholes, which can swallow up streets, rip up motorways, destroy forests and even drag unfortunate bystanders to their deaths. Stories featured include a 2016 case in Ripon, North Yorkshire, when a sinkhole measuring 66ft wide and 30ft deep suddenly appeared right behind a woman's house, while there is also footage from Florida, often called the sinkhole capital of the world(n)

After tracking down the landlord, they have mercy and allow him to spend his £5,000 fine on fixing up the property, in the hope he will turn it into a nice family home. programme.title: The Mysteries of Laura

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Police action to close down an illegal dog-fighting ring leads to a murder inquiry when officers find a woman's finger inside one of the creatures. As the case deepens, Detectives Green and Lupo must contend with the involvement of a pushy journalist. Crime drama, guest starring Lara Flynn Boyle (The Practice)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Lupo and Green investigate when an adulterous art dealer is stoned to death. The detectives learn that the victim's plan to display provocative art led to unwanted attention from a terrorist organisation and gives them cause to suspect her son - a fanatical Christian - was persuaded by a religious leader to kill his mother. Police drama, starring Sam Waterston(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: McCoy and Borgia prosecute a famous basketball player accused of murdering a fan who had been harassing him for some time. They discover the star's fingerprints in the victim's apartment, but the evidence linking him to the crime scene is dismissed as inadmissible because the detectives cut corners to find a match on the police database. Starring Sam Waterston and Annie Parisse(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A car chase through Manhattan leaves one man dead and a teenager injured, leading Fontana and Green to discover the victim was being followed after massacring a hunting party. As the man who led the chase is publicly lauded for his brave efforts, McCoy and Borgia make their case that his poor decisions only endangered other lives. Starring Sam Waterston and Annie Parisse(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A gang leader is stabbed to death in a prison scuffle, and Briscoe and Green uncover a number of suspects as it becomes clear the victim had numerous enemies on both sides of the law. The investigation results in a charge, but McCoy faces an uphill battle to convict the accused. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Jesse L Martin(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a clothes shop owner during an apparent robbery. An open lock suggests someone who works there was responsible, and the trail eventually leads to the son of the dead man's business partner, whose shady dealings were at risk of being exposed. However, the suspect flees to Israel before police have a chance to question him(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Fontana and Green investigate the murder of an undercover reporter who was working on a story about a violent street gang known as L-12, following a trail of clues that leads them to a frightened businessman. Realising that his evidence could be vital to cracking the case, McCoy and Borgia urge him to testify - but he refuses to co-operate for fear of reprisals against his imprisoned son(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A nurse with a social agenda is arrested on suspicion of performing illegal sterilisations on troubled teenagers without their knowledge - a policy that has resulted in the death of one patient. As McCoy and Borgia prepare their case against her, they discover former ADA Paul Robinette is planning to act for the defence(n)

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: The team is unable to trust anyone in the city, so turn to the FBI for help in foiling the mayor's sinister scheme(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A spiritual healer is accused of sexually assaulting a client while they are hypnotised(n)

programme.desc: A young boy is found wandering alone in Central Park and the search for his parents leads to startling evidence of a planned mass casualty event in the city. programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: A man convicted of rape is exonerated and released 16 years later. However, on the night of his release, the daughter of the woman he was convicted of raping is also raped and murdered - and he is SVU's prime suspect. Detective drama, starring Mariska Hargitay(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

The first of a two-part cross-over episode with Chicago PD, guest starring Sophia Bush and Jon Seda(n)

programme.desc: A murderer wages a cyber war on healthcare workers he holds a grudge against. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise and Anna Belknap, with Greg Germann (Ally McBeal)(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A novelist's apparent suicide becomes a murder case, with the evidence leading Lupo and his new partner Kevin Bernard to suspect a secretive religious group. Award-winning police drama, starring Jeremy Sisto and Anthony Anderson(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A celebrity chef is suspected of murdering his lover, a TV network executive who had antagonised him by deciding to cancel his cookery show. The case eventually goes to court, but a juror is accused of trying to profit from the trial. Drama, starring Dennis Farina and Jesse L Martin(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: An ambitious lawyer is murdered in her office and evidence points to one of her colleagues, who was present at the scene. However, a further crime later takes place, prompting McCoy to shift the focus to a senior partner at the law firm who provided the suspect with an alibi. Guest starring Fritz Weaver, with Sam Waterston(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Briscoe and Green investigate the murder of a white police officer, and the trail ultimately leads to a former black rights activist. As the trial gets under way, racial tensions in the city are inflamed with the emergence of evidence linking the dead man to several white supremacist organisations. McCoy realises whichever verdict is returned, it is guaranteed to be greeted with public outrage(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Van Buren orders Briscoe and Green to reinvestigate unsolved cases, starting with the murder of a teenager 20 years previously. However, they discover much of the evidence gathered at the time has vanished, and the detective in charge of the case may have pursued an innocent man to protect the real culprit. An unexpected confession allows the case to end up in court again, but legal trickery makes a conviction an unlikely prospect(n)

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Fontana and Green track down a disturbed woman who has not only murdered a young mother and stolen her five-day-old son, but also managed to convince her own husband that the baby is really theirs. programme.title: Law & Order

However, their only witness is a Hispanic woman who risks deportation if she testifies(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs fights for his life on board a US Navy hospital ship after being shot. Meanwhile, DiNozzo and Joanna Teague head for Shanghai, where they hope to take down the leaders of the terrorist group known as The Calling. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Mimi Rogers(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs throws himself back into his work following his near-death experience, updating his appearance and tackling a new risky case alongside DEA agent Luis Mitchell, who wants help bringing down a drugs cartel. Concerned about his colleague's apparent enthusiasm to re-enter the field, DiNozzo discovers Mitchell is the son of a former NCIS agent who gave his life trying to save Gibbs' late wife and daughter, and this attachment is causing Gibbs to break his own rule against personal involvement in a case. However, a significant danger is posed by Benson Long, the cartel's head, and Mitchell's top target(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Six of Team GB's Beijing gold medal winners pair up to play for £1million in another seasonal special. Half of all winnings will be donated to the British Olympic Association and the other half to charities of each Olympian's choice. Rower Zac Purchase and sailor Sarah Webb answer questions to raise funds for the Dyslexia Association and the Royal National Lifeboat Institution, followed by Paralympic cyclist Darren Kenny and Paralympic swimmer Liz Johnson, appearing on behalf of the Piam Brown Child Cancer Fund and Dreamflight. To end the night, rower Steve Williams and athlete Christine Ohuruogu raise money for their selected charities, Livability and SportsAid(n)
